name: strictdoc

metadata.name: strictdoc
metadata.summary: StrictDoc is open-source software for technical documentation and requirements management.

documentation:
# StrictDoc

![](https://github.com/stanislaw/strictdoc/actions/workflows/ci-mac.yml/badge.svg)
![](https://github.com/stanislaw/strictdoc/actions/workflows/ci-linux-ubuntu-latest.yml/badge.svg)
![](https://github.com/stanislaw/strictdoc/actions/workflows/ci-windows.yml/badge.svg)

The documentation is hosted on Read the Docs:
[StrictDoc documentation](https://strictdoc.readthedocs.io/en/stable/).

modules.name: strictdoc.cli.argument_int_range
documentation: Custom argparse type representing a bounded int

modules.name: strictdoc.cli.main
documentation: Needed to ensure that multiprocessing.freeze_support() is called
in a frozen application (see main() below).

methods.name: strictdoc.backend.sdoc_source_code.models.source_file_info.SourceFileTraceabilityInfo.__init__
documentation:
At the init time, only the backward RangeMarkers are available from
a source file. At runtime, the ForwardRangeMarkers are mixed in
from the Requirement/FileReference links. This is why the .markers
is a union.

methods.name: strictdoc.backend.sdoc_source_code.reader_python.SourceFileTraceabilityReader_Python.get_node_ns
documentation: Walk up the tree and find parent classes

classes.name: strictdoc.backend.sdoc.models.document_view.DefaultViewElement
documentation:
FIXME: It is not great to provide a default implementation this way but it
       works fine for now.

methods.name: strictdoc.backend.sdoc.models.node.SDocNode.set_field_value
documentation:
The purpose of this purpose is to provide a single-method API for
updating any field of a requirement. A requirement might use only some
fields of a document grammar, so an extra exercise done by the method is
to ensure that an added field that has not been attached to the
requirement before will be put at the right index.

classes.name: strictdoc.backend.sdoc.validations.sdoc_validator.SDocValidator
documentation:
This helper class is used for validating SDoc documents right after the
textX parsing and processing steps have finished.
FIXME: In the future, all processing validation steps could be moved to this
       class as well. This would remove the need in the ParseContext class
       and simplify several other implementation details.

methods.name: strictdoc.backend.sdoc.reader.SDReader.read_from_file
documentation:
This function parses the provided .sdoc file and returns a Document
object.

methods.name: strictdoc.core.project_config.ProjectConfig.integrate_server_config
documentation: Some server command settings can override the project config settings.

classes.name: strictdoc.core.graph.validations.RemoveNodeValidation
documentation:
FIXME: This class is not tested. Also, most of the code should rather be
       in the "can perform operation?"-kind of class.

methods.name: strictdoc.core.document_meta.DocumentMeta.__init__
documentation:
Example explaining meta data stored by this class:

DocumentMeta(
    level = 1,
    file_tree_mount_folder = "doc_project",
    document_filename = "sample.sdoc",
    document_filename_base = "sample",
    input_doc_full_path = "/tmp/doc_project/child.sdoc",
    input_doc_rel_path = "child.sdoc",
    input_doc_dir_rel_path = "",
    input_doc_assets_dir_rel_path = "doc_project/_assets",
    output_document_dir_full_path = "/tmp/doc_project/output/html/doc_project",
    output_document_dir_rel_path = "doc_project"
)

classes.name: strictdoc.core.traceability_index.SDocNodeConnections
documentation: FIXME: Rename/refactor to NodeConnections (Requirements and Sections).

methods.name: strictdoc.core.traceability_index.TraceabilityIndex.is_small_project
documentation:
This method helps to decide if StrictDoc will precompile Jinja templates
to Python files or not. Precompilation may take half a second time, so
it is only worth doing it when a project is relatively large.

Below, making some assumptions about what makes a small or larger
project.

methods.name: strictdoc.core.traceability_index.TraceabilityIndex.get_node_by_uid_weak2
documentation: FIXME: This can likely replace _weak below with no problem.

methods.name: strictdoc.core.traceability_index.TraceabilityIndex.update_last_updated
documentation:
This is a rather broad way of signalling that all documents of the index
need to be re-generated when they are opened next time. Several UI
actions use this method to ensure a complete re-generation of all
documents.

methods.name: strictdoc.core.traceability_index.TraceabilityIndex.clone_to_bundle_document
documentation:
The only use case for this method is the generation of a bundle document.
Since the bundle document does not exist on file system, some parameters
are set artificially:
- The bundle is assumed to be an input file in the root input folder.
- The bundle is generated to the root of the output folder (level=0).
- Some variables do not contribute (yet) to the final result, so they
  are marked as NOT_RELEVANT.

functions.name: strictdoc.helpers.file_system.get_etag

This implementation is taken from StackOverflow. The implementation itself
is taken from Starlette.
https://stackoverflow.com/a/76263874/598057

NOTE: The Starlette's version is async and uses anyio.

methods.name: strictdoc.helpers.parallelizer.MultiprocessingParallelizer._run
documentation: This version doesn't handle the following cases properly:
- when a child process exists unexpectedly
- when a child process raises exception

classes.name: strictdoc.helpers.paths.SDocRelativePath
documentation:
This rel path will be used for lookups of URL pages that are
always "/"-based, even on Windows.

classes.name: strictdoc.helpers.ordered_set.OrderedSet
documentation:
A set that preserves insertion order by internally using a dict.

```pycon
>>> OrderedSet([1, 2, "foo"])
```

Source: <https://github.com/bustawin/ordered-set-37>

methods.name: strictdoc.export.html.renderers.link_renderer.LinkRenderer.render_static_url_with_prefix
documentation: This rarely used helper adds slashes to the import statements within
Otherwise, scripts are not imported correctly.

methods.name: strictdoc.export.html.renderers.link_renderer.LinkRenderer.render_node_link
documentation:
allow_local:     used on the DTR screen where we want to ensure that only
                 full paths are used when jumping to the DOC screen.

methods.name: strictdoc.export.html.html_generator.HTMLGenerator.export_assets
documentation:
This is the primary method for copying all assets to the destination/output
folder during HTML and HTML2PDF exports.

:param bool flat_assets: This parameter is always set to False except when
                         exporting a "bundle document" with HTML2PDF.
                         The bundle document contains all documents of
                         the documentation tree. In this case, all assets
                         are simply copied to the top level _assets folder,
                         independently on how nested the contained documents are.

classes.name: strictdoc.export.html.form_objects.requirement_form_object.RequirementFormObject

methods.name: strictdoc.export.html.form_objects.requirement_form_object.RequirementFormObject.create_new
documentation:
For now, the next_uid cannot be non-None for TEXT nodes. This will likely
change in the future.

context_document_mid: The MID of the document where the requirement is edited.
                      Normally, this is the requirement's own document but can
                      also be the parent document if requirement's own document
                      is included to it.
